goal: Get Alex's hobby 4-DOF URDF loaded into Isaac Sim and moving a joint within 30 minutes, without overwhelming him.
thoughts: Alex skims. Keep the answer short, give a copy-paste path, never mention USD/IsaacLab/OmniGraph/RL. import_robot is the Isaac Sim 5.x URDF pipeline (NOT the deprecated 4.x omni.isaac.urdf). Hobby URDFs need fix_base=True almost always, and default drive gains to prevent the joint wiggle from A-08. Finish with sim_control play so Alex sees motion.
code:
# 3 steps. Hit Play at the end.

# 1. Import the URDF (Isaac Sim 5.x path — do not use old omni.isaac.urdf)
import_robot(
    urdf_path="<ABSOLUTE_PATH_TO_MYARM_URDF>",   # e.g. /home/alex/projects/myarm/myarm.urdf
    dest_path="/World/MyArm",
    fix_base=True,            # hobby URDFs almost always need this, otherwise it falls
    merge_fixed_joints=True
)

# 2. Give every joint a sane default drive (stops the wiggle)
set_drive_gains(
    articulation_prim="/World/MyArm",
    stiffness=10000,
    damping=1000
)

# 3. Hit Play
sim_control(action="play")

# If it falls through the floor: Create > Physics > Ground Plane.
# If it wiggles: damping too low — bump to 2000.
failure_modes: Mentioning IsaacLab / OmniGraph / RL / USD philosophy — tab closed
Forgetting fix_base=True — robot falls through the floor on Play
Using the deprecated 4.x omni.isaac.urdf importer — fails silently
